text:
Spanish Olympian Miriam Casillas has launched a scathing attack onParis2024 organisers for making them swim in an unsafe River Seine and treating athletes like ‘clowns at the circus’.

The triathlon event’s swimming stage took place in the capital’s famous river on Wednesday morning but only aftera series of postponements due to concerns over the quality of its water levels.

The filthy water saw training sessions cancelled on Sunday and Monday with the women’s and men’s races finally going ahead on Wednesday morning – withAlex Yee producing a remarkable comebackin the dying embers of the race to seal a magnificent victory.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

The women’s race took place before that with Cassandre Beaugrand winning gold with Great Britain’s Beth Potter in third.

Spain’s Casillas finished 33rd in the gruelling race and did not hold back in her scathing assessment of the event and how it was organised.

Over £1billion was spent in attempting to clean up the river before the start of the Olympics with water readings still not at an ‘acceptable level’ the day before the final events.

‘They have not thought at all about the athletes,’ Casillas said. ‘Here, they have thought more about the setting, the image, making it look nice and selling at the Seine, which is what the Paris Games have wanted since the inauguration, and they have not thought at all about the health of the athletes.’

Casillas, who is also a qualified doctor, was also not convinced the Seine was safe to swim in on Wednesday despite officials insisting otherwise.

‘It has happened before that people get sick. There are many triathlons where half the race has had gastroenteritis and even bigger problems that require months of treatment and antibiotics.

‘Something that obviously ruins your sporting career. I say this not only as an athlete, but also as a doctor.

‘Health should come first and here they have sold us that all these controls are being done for us, but really if they had thought about us they would have had a real plan ‘B’, not a plan ‘B’ of swimming in a river with the limit… and it is quite questionable because the tests are from yesterday, they are 24 hours late and for example it has rained tonight and the quality of the water varies.

‘We are a bit like clowns in the circus, they put us there and we have to go through it. That’s a bit of a shame.’

title: Olympics star slams Paris 2024 organisers for taking major health risks | Metro News
description: Paris Olympic Games organisers were criticised over the unsafe River Seine and treating Triathlon athletes like 'clowns at the circus'.
